text: If you secretly despise wealth, it will elude you.
Making money isn't a thing you do — it's a skill you learn.
Earn with your mind, not your time.
You won't get rich renting out your time. You must own equity to gain your financial freedom.
Technology is not only the thing that moves the human race forward, but it's the only thing that ever has. Without technology, we're just monkeys playing in the dirt.
The secret to public speaking is to speak as if you were alone.
Free education is abundant, all over the Internet. It's the desire to learn that's scarce.
If you eat, invest, and think according to what the 'news' advocates, you'll end up nutritionally, financially, and morally bankrupt.
A busy mind accelerates the perceived passage of time. Buy time by living fully in the moment.
The most important trick to being happy is to realize that happiness is a choice that you make and a skill that you develop. You choose to be happy, and then you work at it.
Happiness, love, and passion are not things you find — they're choices you make.
Suffering is a moment of clarity, when you can no longer deny the truth of a situation and are forced into uncomfortable change.
A rational person can find peace by cultivating indifference to things outside of their control.
To write a great book, you must first become the book.
Smart money is just dumb money that's been through a crash.
You make your own luck if you stay at it long enough.
Productize yourself.
What you choose to work on, and who you choose to work with, are far more important than how hard you work.
Following your genuine intellectual curiosity is a better foundation for a career than following whatever is making money right now.
Intentions don't matter. Actions do. That's why being ethical is hard.
Before you can lie to another, you must first lie to yourself.
Information is everywhere but its meaning is created by the individual.
A taste of freedom can make you unemployable.
All greatness comes from suffering.
People are oddly consistent. Karma is just you, repeating your patterns, virtues, and flaws until you finally get what you deserve.
Total honesty at all times is one of the best tools you'll ever discover.
If you want to make the wrong decision, ask everyone.
A personal metric: how much of the day is spent doing things out of obligation rather than out of interest?

Your real resume is just a catalog of all your suffering. If I ask you to describe your real life to me, and you listed your achievements, I'd say those are great, but I already knew that. Tell me what you've been through, and I'll know what you're capable of.
Knowledge is a skyscraper. You can take a shortcut with a fragile foundation of memorization, or build slowly upon a steel frame of understanding.
The older the problem, the older the solution.
Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
All self-help boils down to: choose long-term over short-term.
If you're not willing to be misunderstood, you'll never do anything original.
An educated, healthy, and free population is the most powerful force on earth.
In a long-term game, it seems like everybody is making money except you. In a short-term game, it seems like you're making money while everybody is losing.
Reading a book isn't a race — the better the book, the more slowly it should be absorbed.
The internet enables any niche interest, as long as you're the best person in the world at it.
A group of people who are free to do what they want usually end up doing what's right.
The measure of wealth is freedom. The measure of health is weightlessness. The measure of intellect is judgment. The measure of wisdom is silence. The measure of love is peace.
To be honest, speak without identity.
Trade money for time, not time for money. You're going to run out of time first.
When everyone is sick, we no longer consider it a disease.
The most dangerous things are heroin and a monthly salary.
Do everything you were going to do, but with less angst, less suffering, less emotion. Everything you were going to do, but with less drama.
Peace is happiness at rest. Happiness is peace in motion.
Caught in a funk? Use meditation, music, and exercise to reset your mood. Then choose a new path to commit emotional energy for rest of day.
My one repeated learning in life: there are no adults. Everyone's making it up as they go along. Figure it out yourself, and do it.
Cynics sound smart. Optimists make money.
Don't take yourself so seriously. You're just a monkey with a plan.
Think clearly from the ground up. Understand and explain from first principles. Ignore society and politics. Acknowledge what you have. Look for meaning and purpose in what you do.
There are basically three really big decisions you make in your early life: where you live, who you're with, and what you do.
We waste our time with short-term thinking and busywork. Warren Buffett spends a year deciding and a day acting. That act lasts decades.
The most powerful people in the world are the ones who can say no.
No one is going to value you more than you value yourself.

Wealth is the thing you want. Wealth is assets that earn while you sleep; wealth is the factory, the robots that's cranking out things. Wealth is the computer program that's running at night, serving other customers. Wealth is money in the bank that is being reinvested into other assets and other businesses.
Play stupid games, win stupid prizes.
You should be too busy to do coffee, while keeping an uncluttered calendar.
Successful people are just unsuccessful people who kept going.

The modern struggle: Lone individuals summoning inhuman willpower, fasting, meditating, and exercising… using cold showers, psychedelics, and extreme sports… leveraging social media, artificial intelligence, and virtual reality… to break free of the matrix.
